top_findings.title: Server panics on startup — duplicate ServeMux route registration
top_findings.evidence: main.go:28-29 register "/books" twice on http.NewServeMux(); confirmed at runtime: panic: pattern "/books" conflicts with pattern "/books" from net/http (*ServeMux).register. main() never reaches ListenAndServe. /books/{id} is also registered 3x.
top_findings.suggestion: Build the production router the same way the tests do — a single gorilla/mux router with one HandleFunc per (path, method) using .Methods(). The panic makes the primary deliverable non-functional.

top_findings.title: main.go uses stdlib ServeMux but handlers require gorilla/mux (broken even without the panic)
top_findings.evidence: main.go:22 uses http.NewServeMux() with no method filter, while handler/book_handler.go:41,95,138 read path params via mux.Vars(r). Under stdlib ServeMux mux.Vars returns empty, so id parsing fails and GET/POST/PUT/DELETE on the same path cannot be dispatched.
top_findings.suggestion: Use gorilla/mux in main.go (router.HandleFunc(...).Methods(...)) exactly as test/api_test.go does.
